finding: Blind security/authz/info-disclosure pass: cross-user/cross-conversation leakage NOT possible (double ownership scoping: model_authored_file_ids filters user_id + conversation provenance, get_by_ids_and_user re-filters user_id; assert_owns_conversation still gates dispatch). map_tool_error keeps all 5xx (host-path io_err) generic, surfaces only 4xx (no host path). No injection (filename validated by canonicalize_in_workspace; bytes loaded by blob_version_id, not a model-built path).

New DB calls run inside the existing per-conversation Mutex; no lock-ordering inversion, no deadlock. edit_file load-then-write has no TOCTOU (all non-execute_command tools serialize under the same lock).

list_files deduped model-authored artifacts only against WORKSPACE names, not against each other nor against user attachments (ctx.files). Two same-named artifacts produced two identical 'name' rows that read_file then rejects as AMBIGUOUS; and a name that is both an attachment and a same-named artifact showed the artifact entry while read_file returns the attachment. list/read inconsistency.
(same as the state-management finding, independently confirmed) list_files artifact-vs-artifact dedup gap contradicts the loop's own 'an artifact you can read is also listed' comment.

AMBIGUOUS_FILENAME (artifact) message said 'via the `files` MCP `read_file` by its id (from its list_files)' — imprecise (the model sees no server literally named 'files'; both code_sandbox and files_mcp expose read_file/list_files) and the '(from its list_files)' pronoun is loose.

list_files comment 'Reuses the SAME model-authored source as the read path (no drift)' overstated: read path is workspace->attachment->artifact but list only deduped artifacts against workspace, so an attachment-vs-artifact name collision diverged.

list_files now issues the model_authored_file_ids query on EVERY call (even zero-artifact conversations), inside the per-conversation lock — previously pure filesystem. ACCEPTED: list must enumerate artifacts to surface them; the query is a single indexed provenance lookup and mirrors the reviewed files-MCP manifest, which does the same on every list. Bounded by conversation artifact count; no cheap skip exists without a second query.

conversation_model_authored_files hydrates the full model-authored set via get_by_ids_and_user with no LIMIT. ACCEPTED: identical to the reviewed resolve_available_files pairing (available_files.rs:576); a conversation's artifact set is small in practice and bounded; adding pagination would diverge from the sibling manifest and is out of scope for this fix.

The attachment AMBIGUOUS message (cat suffixed paths) and the artifact AMBIGUOUS message (files read_file by id) give different remediation for the same code. ACCEPTED: each is locally correct — attachments ARE bind-mounted (cat works), artifacts are NOT — so the mechanisms genuinely differ; unifying them would give wrong advice for one case.

TEST-5 (workspace-first) was paper-green: load_file_content returns the workspace file before reaching the new fallback, so the test passed with the diff fully reverted (it guarded pre-existing ordering, not the new path).
TEST-9 (ambiguity) asserted only code==-32602 + contains 'dup.csv' + != generic — all also true of FILE_NOT_FOUND, so it did not positively verify the AMBIGUOUS_FILENAME branch.
enabled_server() created a per-test placeholder rootfs dir under temp_dir (cs-artifacts-rootfs-<uuid>) and never removed it — a dir leaks per server boot.

Fix faithfully mirrors PR #152: reuses model_authored_file_ids + get_by_ids_and_user, map_tool_error delegates to the shared from_app_error (like files_mcp/memory_mcp) with a deliberate 5xx->generic guard. No reinvention, no drift. test-reality: TEST-1/2/3/4/6/7 fail on revert; upload_and_promote files reach the tool ONLY via the new model-authored path (ctx.files never surfaces them).

JSON-RPC response/error envelope shape unchanged; the only behavioral shift is error CLASS (existing tool failures now -32602/-32601 with real messages instead of -32603 'tool X failed') — the intended improvement, not a regression. UNKNOWN_TOOL->method_not_found(-32601) pass-through acceptable.

Fallback error paths correctly classified: load_original failure -> io_err (500, hidden by map_tool_error); non-UTF-8 -> BINARY_FILE (400); genuine miss -> clean 404 FILE_NOT_FOUND (no host path). The non-NotFound workspace IO arm stays a 500 io_err. Consistent with the attachment branch.

No new permission introduced. Ownership is double-scoped (model_authored_file_ids user_id + get_by_ids_and_user user_id) and assert_owns_conversation still gates dispatch before build_context. The widened file set grants nothing beyond what the conversation owner already owns.
